title: Use of OGC WKT literals
description:
Is it sufficient enough to use Virtuoso geosparql operators if the only portion of geosparql I use are the WKT literals?
Example data
_:oddthing private:weirdthing "POINT(2 2)"^^geo:wktLiteral .

Would then, a query like this work, as in _:oddthing would be selected  by the filter?
PREFIX geo: <http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#>
PREFIX geof: <http://www.opengis.net/def/function/geosparql/>
PREFIX private: <http://dontknow.com/ns/>

SELECT ?s WHERE {
  ?s private:weirdthing ?wkt .
  FILTER(geof:distance(?wkt, "POINT(0 0)"^^geo:wktLiteral) < 10)
}
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE geospatial_data (id INT PRIMARY KEY, location POINT, description VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO geospatial_data (id, location, description) VALUES (1, POINT(2, 2), 'Odd Thing');
INSERT INTO geospatial_data (id, location, description) VALUES (2, POINT(5, 5), 'Another Thing');
SELECT id FROM geospatial_data WHERE ST_Distance(location, POINT(0, 0)) < 10;
SELECT id FROM geospatial_data WHERE location IS NOT NULL AND ST_Distance(location, POINT(3, 3)) < 5;
key_bug_features: FILTER with geof:distance on WKT literals
